title: Orchestra run state
description: STATE.json — the single source of truth AND the commit point for a run. Written last and atomically (DESIGN §10). The orchestrator is a pure function of this file plus the blackboard. Every external call has a persisted phase on both sides so any crash resumes to the correct next action.

started_at.description: When pipeline execution began; basis for the wall-clock budget.

updated_at.description: Bumped on every state write; orchestra status flags staleness (possible hang).

status.description: Persisted phase. authoring/reviewing are written BEFORE the subprocess call (idempotent retry on resume). authored records artifact written/pending review. deciding is written after review with last_verdict, before branching. See DESIGN §8.

waiting_for.description: Discriminator when status == awaiting_human: are we blocked on a human approval/iterate decision, or on answers.md? Null otherwise.

stuck_reason.description: Set when status == stuck; lets orchestra status distinguish a cheap fundamental REJECT from an expensive burned-out loop. 'monitor' = the supervisory overseer intercepted (DESIGN §10.1). Null otherwise.

round.description: Per-stage, resets to 0 on stage entry. Round 0 = initial author draft (pre-review). Round k (>=1) = the k-th review + the revise that produced the reviewed artifact. On resume, derived from history's max round for the current stage.

gate.description: Human-in-loop level for the current stage. Reset to the new stage's configured tier on stage advance.

attempts.description: Retry attempts for the current in-flight call (retry/backoff accounting, DESIGN §10).

tokens_spent.description: Accumulated output tokens (Claude usage + Codex); checked against config.budget.tokens (DESIGN §9).

min_confidence.description: Per-stage APPROVE confidence floor; below it an APPROVE downgrades to awaiting_human. 0 disables.

reviewer.description: Codex model id, or null to use Codex's configured default.

call_timeout_seconds.description: Per-subprocess timeout; a hung child is killed.

last_verdict.description: The most recent verdict object (see verdict.schema.json), or null.

artifact.description: Path to the round's artifact snapshot.

artifact_hash.description: Content hash recorded at 'authored', so resume knows the draft is committed.
